Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall represent Digimon entities uniquely identified by name and number.
The ontology shall represent the evolutionary stage of each Digimon.
Comments (optional): Stage classification is required for lifecycle modeling.

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall represent the type classification of each Digimon and support retrieving; grouping and comparing Digimon according to their type in order to reason about combat suitability in the battle team planning use case.
Comments (optional): Expanded to explicitly connect Digimon type with grouping; comparison and reasoning tasks required in the battle-oriented use case.
Relation with other requirements (option: Related to FR4; FR7; FR23 and Use Case 1.

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall represent the attribute associated with each Digimon and support retrieving and comparing Digimon by attribute to reason about their effectiveness in different battle scenarios.
Comments (optional): Expanded to better align attribute information with use case reasoning tasks and comparison queries.
Relation with other requirements (option: Related to FR3; FR7; FR23 and Use Case 1.

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall represent the memory usage value of each Digimon.
The ontology shall represent the equipment slots available for each Digimon.
The ontology shall represent the base statistical values of Digimon at level 50 (HP; SP; Attack; Defense; Intelligence; Speed).
Extracted from (provenance): DigiDB_digimonlist.csv (Lv 50 HP; Lv50 SP; Lv50 Atk; Lv50 Def; Lv50 Int; Lv50 Spd)
Associated ontology elements: DatatypeProperty: hasHP | DatatypeProperty: hasSP | DatatypeProperty: hasAttack | DatatypeProperty: hasDefense | DatatypeProperty: hasIntelligence | DatatypeProperty: hasSpeed

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall represent Move entities including their name and description.
The ontology shall represent the SP cost of each Move.
The ontology shall represent the power value of each Move.
The ontology shall represent whether a Move is inheritable.
The ontology shall represent Support Skill entities including their name and description.
The ontology shall allow linking Digimon with their Moves.
Comments (optional): Necessary to model which Digimon can use which Moves.
Extracted from (provenance): Conceptual modeling decision based on DigiDB datasets

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall represent Skill entities from the extended dataset.
Comments (optional): Cyber Sleuth introduces additional skill structures.

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall represent the relationship between Digimon and the Skills they can learn.
The ontology shall represent Digivolution as an entity describing the evolutionary process.
The ontology shall represent the source Digimon involved in a Digivolution.
The ontology shall represent the target Digimon resulting from a Digivolution.
The ontology shall represent Digivolution requirements associated with each Digivolution.
The ontology shall represent numerical or conditional constraints required for Digivolution processes.
Comments (optional): Allows modeling level-based and stat-based requirements.
Associated ontology elements: DatatypeProperty: hasMinLevel | DatatypeProperty: hasMinHP | DatatypeProperty: hasMinSP | DatatypeProperty: hasMinAttack | DatatypeProperty: hasMinDefense | DatatypeProperty: hasMinIntelligence | DatatypeProperty: hasMinSpeed

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall allow querying complete evolution chains between Digimon.
Comments (optional): Enables reasoning over multi-step evolution paths.

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall allow retrieving all Skills associated with a given Digimon.

The ontology shall allow identifying; grouping and comparing Digimon that share common types; attributes; skills or support skills; enabling more complex queries required in the battle team planning use case.
Comments (optional): Expanded to include relevant dataset aspects that were not sufficiently reflected before; especially skills and support skills.
Extracted from (provenance): DigiDB_digimonlist.csv (Type; Attribute) | Skills by Digimon.csv | DigiDB_supportlist.csv
Relation with other requirements (option: Related to FR3; FR4; FR12; FR14; FR15; FR22 and Use Case 1.
Associated ontology elements: ObjectProperty: hasType | ObjectProperty: hasAttribute | ObjectProperty: learnsSkill | ObjectProperty: hasSupportSkill

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall allow combining statistical values; skills; support skills and digivolution requirements in queries to support complex reasoning tasks across battle planning and digivolution analysis use cases.
Comments (optional): Expanded to include additional relevant dataset aspects and to make its role in reasoning tasks more explicit.
Extracted from (provenance): Combined DigiDB and Cyber Sleuth datasets
Relation with other requirements (option: Related to FR7; FR12; FR19; FR20; FR23; FR25 and both use cases.
Associated ontology elements: Class: Digimon | Class: Skill | Class: SupportSkill | Class: DigivolutionRequirement | DatatypeProperty: hasHP | DatatypeProperty: hasSP | DatatypeProperty: hasAttack | DatatypeProperty: hasDefense | DatatypeProperty: hasIntelligence | DatatypeProperty: hasSpeed | ObjectProperty: learnsSkill | ObjectProperty: hasSupportSkill | ObjectProperty: hasRequirement

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall support the integration of multiple datasets within the Digimon domain; allowing unified access to Digimon; moves; skills; support skills; digivolutions and digivolution requirements.
Comments (optional): Expanded to better justify dataset extension and interoperability needs in the final assignment.
Relation with other requirements (option: Related to FR8-FR24 and both use cases.
Associated ontology elements: Class: Digimon | Class: Move | Class: Skill | Class: SupportSkill | Class: Digivolution | Class: DigivolutionRequirement | ObjectProperty: usesMove | ObjectProperty: learnsSkill | ObjectProperty: hasSupportSkill | ObjectProperty: digivolvesTo | ObjectProperty: hasRequirement

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall represent the type of each Move as a separate classification; allowing queries and comparisons between Digimon characteristics and move characteristics in battle scenarios.
Comments (optional): Added to generate an additional class and relation from an existing dataset field and to improve the exploitation of the move dataset.
Relation with other requirements (option: Related to FR8; FR9; FR10 and Use Case 1.

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall represent the relationship between Digimon and their support skills to support team composition; comparison and ability-based queries.
Comments (optional): Added to better reflect the relevance of support skill information in the ontology and in the use cases.
Relation with other requirements (option: Related to FR12; FR23 and Use Case 1.

Competency Question / Natural language s: The ontology shall support queries that combine information from DigiDB and the additional Digimon datasets to reason jointly over Digimon stats; moves; skills; support skills and digivolution chains.
Comments (optional): Added to make cross-dataset integration explicit and align the requirements with the extended data sources used in the project.
Extracted from (provenance): DigiDB_digimonlist.csv | DigiDB_movelist.csv | DigiDB_supportlist.csv | Skills.csv | Skills by Digimon.csv | Digivolutions.csv | Digivolution Requirements.csv
Relation with other requirements (option: Related to FR7; FR12; FR14; FR19; FR23; FR24; FR25 and both use cases.
